patterns_discovered: Consistent JSON structure across all three preset files
Uniform key ordering: name → description → language → style → genre → mood → tempo → lyric_themes → structural_tags
All presets use pt-BR language identifier
Naming convention: kebab-case identifiers (brazilian-catholic, brazilian-catholic-christmas, brazilian-catholic-marian)
Theme-specific mood assignments: devotional (base), joyful (Christmas), contemplative (Marian)
Theme-specific tempo variations: moderate (general and Christmas), slow (Marian)

approach_outcome.notes: All three Brazilian Catholic preset JSON files successfully created following Preset schema Pattern 6 with no implementation issues

recommendations: Implement JSON schema validation in preset loading to catch structural deviations early
Add documentation specifying required vs optional keys in preset schema
Consider creating preset template/generator to reduce manual duplication
Add preset file validation test suite to CI pipeline
Document cultural/linguistic considerations for preset creation (e.g., language code standards)
